PHASE: (fresh-client-presence-snapshot-race) + (socketprovider-strictmode-reconnect) bundled and shipped 2026-05-22. Branch `feat/fresh-client-presence-snapshot-race` ready for squash-merge to main after this commit. Single Opus 4.7 inline orchestration (~135K context across two sessions: prior session implemented + unit-tested the `presence:ready` handshake server gate; this session smoke-verified via Playwright multi-context rig and uncovered + fixed the masking `SocketProvider` StrictMode disconnect bug). Verification end-to-end: pre-fix smoke showed `SocketProvider.socket.connected: false` + `sendBuffer.length: 2` on fresh-context clients; the 2 buffered emits were the `presence:ready` handshakes waiting for a connect that never came due to React 18 StrictMode double-fire calling `socket.disconnect()` in cleanup between mounts (Socket.IO's `reconnectionAttempts: 5` budget covers transport drops only, NOT explicit `.disconnect()`). Manual `socket.connect()` from probe restored connection and Reception+Sales tiles immediately matched fixture bindings — confirming the presence:ready fix is correct but masked. Shipped `ensureSocketConnected(socket)` pure helper (apps/web/src/lib/socket/ensure-connected.ts + ensure-connected.test.ts, 3 RED→GREEN tests, per [[pure-helper-extraction-pattern]]); invoked at top of SocketProvider's useEffect. Post-fix smoke (fresh login, no force-connect): alice + bob both `socketConnected: true` automatically, `sendBufferLen: 0`, all 4 Speed Dial tiles match fixture bindings (Reception online + Sales online + Front Desk offline + Support offline) on every fresh client. **#16 dept-filter UNBLOCKED end-to-end.** **#14 + #15 STILL BLOCKED by THIRD INDEPENDENT BUG** discovered during same smoke: bob's `trpc.departments.myBoundDepartmentIds.useQuery()` in `IncomingCallDialog` is stuck in `status: "pending", isSuccess: false, dataUpdatedAt: 0` indefinitely despite server returning 200 with correct payload `["cmpfmyva2000fa8k1drezs7bw"]` via plain `fetch('/api/trpc/...', { credentials: 'include' })` from inside bob's evaluate context. Manual `queryClient.getQueryCache().getAll().filter(q => q.queryKey.includes('myBound'))[0].fetch()` throws `TypeError: client[procedureType] is not a function at @trpc/client/dist/index.mjs:184:31`. tRPC client proxy is missing one of `query` / `mutation` / `subscription` dispatcher methods. Filed as `(trpc-client-procedure-type-missing)` Tier 1-2 queue item; blocks #14 + #15 until fixed. Versions: @trpc/client@11.17.0 + @trpc/server@11.17.0 + typescript@5.9.3.

PORTS: APP=43512 DB=43502 CACHE=43504 MINIO=43505 MINIO_CONSOLE=43506 SMTP=43507 SMTP_UI=43508 PGADMIN=43509 LIVEKIT=43532 LIVEKIT_UDP=43537 COTURN=43542 SOCKET=43515

LESSONS_ADDED: 🔴 [[strictmode-socket-disconnect-permanent]] — SocketProvider's useEffect cleanup calls socket.disconnect(); Socket.IO does NOT auto-reconnect after explicit .disconnect() (its reconnectionAttempts:5 is transport-drop only). Under reactStrictMode:true dev double-fire, the second mount attaches listeners to a permanently-dead socket. HMR + route remounts hit same shape. Fix pattern: extract `ensureSocketConnected(socket)` pure helper, invoke at top of provider effect. Diagnostic signature: `socket.connected===false + socket.disconnected===true + engine.readyState==="closed" + backoff.attempts===0 + sendBuffer.length>0`. Cross-references [[csp-dev-cross-port-socket-blocked]] (sibling: that was CSP-blocked transport, this is post-cleanup-explicit-disconnect — both share symptom shape "socket on but presence empty"); [[pure-helper-extraction-pattern]] (the extraction template).

🔴 [[trpc-client-procedure-type-missing]] — `trpc.departments.myBoundDepartmentIds.useQuery()` in IncomingCallDialog is stuck status:pending indefinitely despite the endpoint returning 200 with correct payload via plain fetch(). queryCache refetch throws `TypeError: client[procedureType] is not a function at @trpc/client/dist/index.mjs:184`. Cascade: boundDeptIds === undefined → selectIncomingCall returns false → IncomingCallDialog never opens → blocks #14 + #15. Other tRPC queries in SpeedDial apparently work — bug is procedure-shape-specific or component-mount-context-specific. Versions: @trpc/client@11.17.0 + @trpc/server@11.17.0 + react-query v5 + typescript@5.9.3. DO NOT "fix" by removing the boundDeptIds undefined guard — that would let arbitrary tenants ring random users when query is loading.
